stimulus:
[Line graph titled “Coffee Production for Three Countries, 1965–1995.” Vertical axis: yearly coffee production in thousands of metric tons. Horizontal axis: 1965, 1980, 1995.]

  • Brazil: 1965 ~1,500; 1980 ~1,900; 1995 ~2,200
  • Vietnam: 1965 ~30; 1980 ~80; 1995 ~220
  • Colombia: 1965 ~470; 1980 ~700; 1995 ~820

Coffee had long been a popular beverage, but global demand surged after the mid-twentieth century. Improvements in irrigation, processing equipment, and international trade routes helped several countries dramatically increase how much coffee they grew. Several countries saw rapid growth in the production of this resource, for example: ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses the data in the graph to complete the example?

answerOptions.content: Colombia’s coffee production rose more slowly from 1980 to 1995 than from 1965 to 1980.
Coffee production rose significantly from 1965 to 1995 in Brazil, Vietnam, and Colombia.
Brazil produced more coffee than Colombia and Vietnam combined in 1995.
Fewer than 100 thousand metric tons of coffee were produced in Vietnam in 1965.

rationale:
Choice B is correct because the claim asks for examples of countries that experienced rapid growth in coffee production. Choice B identifies all three countries shown as having significant growth from 1965 to 1995.

Choice A is incorrect: it discusses only one country and emphasizes slowdown, not rapid growth. Choice C is incorrect: it compares producers in a single year, not growth over time. Choice D is incorrect: it discusses only one country in a single year, not rapid growth.
